macro_events.event_summary: Hot inflation report causes markets to price in higher odds of a Fed rate hike, reversing the easing trajectory.
primary_drivers: Higher discount rate compresses long-duration equity valuations
Wider net interest margin for banks as front-end rates rise
USD strength pressures multinational and commodity-linked names

beneficiaries.rationale: Large-cap money-center bank with asset-sensitive balance sheet; higher front-end rates expand NIM on floating-rate loans before deposit beta catches up.
Charles Schwab earns substantial net interest revenue on client cash sweep balances; higher rates directly lift spread income and reduce cash-sorting risk relative to prior cuts narrative.
CME Group benefits from elevated rate volatility — SOFR futures, Treasury futures, and FX volume surge when Fed path is repriced; also earns float on margin deposits at higher rates.

Moody's and rating agencies see surging structured product and refinancing activity stalls under higher rates, but earn fees on volatility-driven issuance shifts; secondary beneficiary via rate-sensitive analytics demand.

losers.rationale: Zillow's revenue is directly tied to home transaction volumes; a hike-back-on scenario freezes the housing market and crushes Premier Agent and mortgage origination revenue.
High-multiple software name trading at >50x sales; long-duration cash flows get repriced most aggressively when terminal rate assumption rises.
Rocket Companies is a pure-play mortgage originator; higher rates kill refi volumes which already collapsed, and slow purchase originations further.

headlines: Markets raise chances for a Fed rate hike following hot inflation report

macro_events.event_summary: Boeing's monthly aircraft deliveries showed a pickup, signaling improving production ramp and cash flow recovery.
primary_drivers: Higher delivery cadence converts inventory to cash for Boeing
Increased 737/787 build rates pull through supplier revenue recognition
Improved confidence in commercial aerospace recovery cycle

beneficiaries.rationale: Direct beneficiary — deliveries are the primary cash recognition event for Boeing; pickup directly improves FCF trajectory and supports the deleveraging narrative.
GE Aerospace's CFM (JV with Safran) supplies LEAP engines exclusively on 737 MAX; higher delivery cadence drives engine shipments and lucrative aftermarket service annuity.
Hexcel supplies composite materials for 787 (and A350); higher 787 delivery rate translates to higher composite shipset consumption.
Howmet makes engine components and fasteners with high content per Boeing airframe; benefits from build-rate increase across 737 and 787 programs.
TransDigm has proprietary content on Boeing platforms; delivery acceleration boosts OEM revenue (though aftermarket is the bigger driver) and validates commercial aero recovery.
Collins Aerospace (RTX) supplies avionics, interiors, and systems on Boeing platforms; delivery pickup pulls through OEM revenue, though smaller exposure than GE.

headlines: Here's what the latest pickup in Boeing deliveries means for investors

macro_events.event_summary: MarketWatch warns that an ongoing oil crisis could escalate into a full-blown catastrophe within a month, implying a sharp near-term spike in crude prices.
primary_drivers: Crude price spike lifts E&P realized prices and cash flow
Higher input costs squeeze fuel-consuming industries (airlines, chemicals, transports)

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream production; every $10/bbl move in Brent adds several billion to annual operating cash flow. Direct leverage to a crude spike.
Permian-heavy pure-play E&P with high oil cut (~80%) and operational leverage; Buffett-backed name typically leads on crude rallies.
Diamondback is a low-cost Permian pure-play oil producer; earnings highly geared to WTI with limited hedge book.
High-beta E&P with international (Egypt, North Sea) exposure that benefits disproportionately from Brent dislocations during supply crises.
Oilfield services demand and pricing power rise with sustained high crude as producers accelerate completions; Halliburton is most US-shale-levered of the big three.
Offshore supply vessel operator with day rates directly tied to offshore drilling activity that reignites at sustained $90+ crude.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs; Southwest discontinued its hedging program, leaving it most exposed among US airlines to a crude spike.
American Airlines runs unhedged on fuel and carries heavy debt load — crude spike directly compresses margins with no offset.
Delta owns Trainer refinery which partially offsets, but jet fuel still dominates cost structure; consensus EPS cuts likely on sustained spike.
Ethylene/polyethylene producer with naphtha and natural gas liquids feedstock costs that track crude; margin compression in a supply-shock scenario.
Freight broker whose trucking partners face diesel surcharge friction; demand destruction from high fuel hits volumes.
Cruise operator with bunker fuel as major cost line and discretionary consumer demand sensitive to gasoline prices eating travel budgets.
Consumer staples retailer faces both diesel-driven logistics cost inflation and lower-income consumer wallet share loss to gas pump — though offset by trade-down dynamic.

headlines: Why the oil crisis could become a full-blown catastrophe within a month
